topic: Poetry Anthology: Power and Conflict / Love and Relationships
subject: english-literature

items.correct: When a speaker says one thing but means another — typically the opposite — relying on shared context for the listener to detect the gap.
items.misconception: Verbal irony is just saying something unexpected.
items.random_wrong: Verbal irony is when you make a joke about something.
items.why: Students conflate irony with surprise or humour. Irony specifically involves a gap between literal and intended meaning — a more precise device than 'unexpected'.

items.q: What did Romantic poets value in nature?
items.correct: Nature as a source of spiritual insight and emotional truth, accessed through imagination — not picturesque scenery for its own sake.
items.misconception: Romantic poets just liked to write about pretty landscapes.
items.random_wrong: Romantic poets believed nature was full of fairies and elves.
items.why: The Romantic engagement with nature was philosophical — it was a route to truth, the sublime and the self. Reducing it to 'pretty scenery' misses Wordsworth's epistemology entirely.

items.q: Why distinguish the speaker of a poem from the poet?
items.correct: The speaker is the voice constructed in the poem; the poet is the writer. Treating them as identical can mislead, especially with dramatic monologues or personae.
items.misconception: The speaker of a poem is always the poet themselves.
items.random_wrong: The speaker is whoever reads the poem out loud.
items.why: Browning's 'My Last Duchess' is not Browning — it is a murderous Duke. Students collapse speaker into poet, losing the persona and the moral complexity that comes with it.
Correct answer: Browning. “Tennyson” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what war was the Light Brigade?
items.why: It's Crimean. “Boer” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year was Ozymandias published?
items.why: The real figure is 1818 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
